faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about FPMI Courses

whatIs.a: FPMI Courses is a platform created by students for students at the Faculty of Applied Mathematics and Informatics (FPMI) at the Technical University of Sofia. It offers collaboratively edited notes, wiki pages for courses, assignments, resources, past exams, and discussion forums.

whoCanUse.a: Anyone can browse content as a guest. Students can register to create and edit wiki pages, post in forums, rate professors, and submit reports or suggestions.

howContribute.a: Simply register and open any wiki page for editing. Make your changes, add a brief summary (what and why you changed), and submit. Your edit will be reviewed by a moderator before being published.

howApproval.a: When you edit a page, the new version awaits moderator approval. The moderator reviews the diff between the old and new version, then can approve or reject the change. This ensures content quality while allowing any student to contribute.

whatIsWiki.a: Wiki pages are the primary way to write notes for courses. They support Markdown formatting with the ability to add tables, code, images, and links.

whatCourses.a: The platform covers courses from FPMI specialties, grouped into categories: Programming, Mathematics, Data Analytics, AI, Databases, Networks, and Cybersecurity.

professorInfo.q: Can I find information about professors?
professorInfo.a: Yes, in the "Professors" section you'll find profiles with office, email, bio, and the courses they teach. You can also rate and leave a review for a professor.

hasForum.a: Yes, each course has its own forum where you can ask questions, discuss materials, and help each other. Each wiki page has a "Discuss" button that leads to the corresponding forum thread.

howReport.q: How can I report an issue or give a suggestion?
howReport.a: Use the contact/support form, available to registered users. You can choose a type: suggestion, feature request, bug report, spam report, or other.

howReportContent.q: How do I report inappropriate content?
howReportContent.a: Every wiki page, forum post, and review has a "Report" button that automatically fills in the type as "spam" and a link to the content. Moderators will review your report.

isOpenSource.a: Yes, the entire codebase is MIT-licensed and available on GitHub. Anyone can fork the project, self-host it, or contribute code.

howContributeDev.q: How can I contribute as a developer?
howContributeDev.a: You can open an issue on GitHub or create a pull request (fork → branch → PR). CI checks lint, types, tests, and build. See CONTRIBUTING.md for local setup instructions.

howBecomeModerator.q: How can I become a moderator?
howBecomeModerator.a: If you're an active member of the community and would like to help moderate content, you can reach out to the admin team directly through the platform's contact form, or send an email to the administrators. Your contributions and activity on the platform will be reviewed before granting moderator access.
